_note: Updated by the game director after each task. Plain language — no jargon.

priorities.title: Lock the design pillars for Ashen Hollow so every decision has a clear filter
priorities.note: Without defined pillars, creative decisions accumulate inconsistently. Every room, mechanic, and story beat needs to pass the same three to five questions. Getting these locked is the highest-value creative task right now.
priorities.proposed_solution: Publish three to five written design pillars every creative decision must pass before more content is approved.

priorities.title: Define the player's emotional arc from first room to final boss
priorities.note: The macro arc determines how abilities, rooms, and story beats are sequenced. Without it, individual design decisions can't be assessed for pacing.
priorities.proposed_solution: Document the player’s emotional arc from first room to final boss to sequence abilities, beats, and pacing checks.

priorities.title: Agree on what v1.0 of Ashen Hollow contains — and what it doesn't
priorities.note: Scope ceiling needs a founder decision. An unfinished game breaks the argument that the tools work. This is a joint decision with the founder.
priorities.proposed_solution: Secure founder sign-off on a hard Ashen Hollow v1.0 scope ceiling and record it in the design decision log.

priorities.title: Establish genre positioning — what Ashen Hollow inherits from the metroidvania tradition and where it departs
priorities.note: Needed before marketing can communicate differentiation and before level design can establish room design conventions.
priorities.proposed_solution: Write genre positioning—what you inherit from metroidvanias and where you diverge—for marketing and level design to cite.

risks_p1.title: No agreed scope ceiling for Ashen Hollow v1.0
risks_p1.note: Scope creep is the most likely thing to prevent the game from shipping. Needs a founder decision before any more rooms or systems are added.

founder_decisions.title: What is in Ashen Hollow v1.0 — and what isn't?
founder_decisions.note: How many biomes, rooms, and mechanics make a shippable first version? This needs a hard ceiling, not a roadmap.

actions.name: Pillar Review
actions.description: Assess a proposed feature, room, mechanic, or content element against each design pillar — passes / conflicts / requires revision.
actions.trigger: Any proposed element before it enters development

actions.name: Player Arc Review
actions.description: Pacing assessment — does this milestone or decision serve the designed emotional arc from opening to final boss?
actions.trigger: Any macro-design milestone or pacing decision

actions.name: Creative Direction Memo
actions.description: Issue a memo defining creative intent for a specific element where drift or ambiguity has been observed.
actions.trigger: A domain is drifting from the creative vision or a new element needs alignment
